steps.title: Compare the two sentences around the blank
steps.content: Sentence 1 makes a general claim: Latimer "improved upon Thomas Edison's design for the electric light bulb." Sentence 2 names the particular improvement: he "made the light bulb more durable by placing cardboard around its carbon filament." Sentence 2 is a specific instance of the general claim in sentence 1.

steps.title: Choose a transition that signals general-to-specific
steps.content: "Specifically" introduces a particular detail that elaborates a previous general statement, which is exactly the relationship here.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Soon,’: signals temporal sequence (shortly after), but sentence 2 is not a later event, it is the same improvement explained. Wrong relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Regardless,’: signals dismissal of preceding info, but sentence 2 builds on sentence 1 rather than disregarding it. Wrong relationship. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘However,’: signals contrast, but sentence 2 agrees with and elaborates sentence 1 instead of opposing it. Wrong relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: signals that what follows is the particular form of the general improvement just mentioned. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
